source: CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
title: Ensure Container-Optimized OS (cos_containerd) is used for GKE node images

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
input_row.title: Ensure Container-Optimized OS (cos_containerd) is used for GKE node images
input_row.description: Use Container-Optimized OS (cos_containerd) as a managed, optimized and hardened base OS that limits the host's attack surface.
input_row.rationale: COS is an operating system image for Compute Engine VMs optimized for running containers. With COS, the containers can be brought up on Google Cloud Platform quickly, efficiently, and securely. Using COS as the node image provides the following benefits: • Run containers out of the box: COS instances come pre-installed with the container runtime and cloud-init. With a COS instance, the container can be brought up at the same time as the VM is created, with no on-host setup required. • Smaller attack surface: COS has a smaller footprint, reducing the instance's potential attack surface. • Locked-down by default: COS instances include a locked-down firewall and other security settings by default. Impact: If modifying an existing cluster's Node pool to run COS, the upgrade operation used is long-running and will block other operations on the cluster (including delete) until it has run to completion. COS nodes also provide an option with containerd as the main container runtime directly integrated with Kubernetes instead of docker. Thus, on these nodes, Docker cannot view or access containers or images managed by Kubernetes. Applications should not interact with Docker directly. For general troubleshooting or debugging, use crictl instead.
input_row.audit: Using Google Cloud Console: 1. Go to Kubernetes Engine by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/kubernetes/list. 2. From the list of clusters, select the cluster under test. 3. Under the 'Node pools' section, make sure that for each of the Node pools, 'Container-Optimized OS (cos_containerd)' is listed in the 'Image type' column. Using Command line: To check Node image type for an existing cluster's Node pool: gcloud container node-pools describe <node_pool_name> --cluster <cluster_name> --zone <compute_zone> --format json | jq '.config.imageType' The output of the above command returns COS_CONTAINERD, if COS_CONTAINERD is used for Node images.
input_row.remediation: Using Google Cloud Console: 1. Go to Kubernetes Engine by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/kubernetes/list. 2. Select the Kubernetes cluster which does not use COS. 3. Under the Node pools heading, select the Node Pool that requires alteration. 4. Click EDIT. 5. Under the Image Type heading click CHANGE. 6. From the pop-up menu select Container-optimised OS with containerd (cos_containerd) (default) and click CHANGE 7. Repeat for all non-compliant Node pools. Using Command Line: To set the node image to cos for an existing cluster's Node pool: gcloud container clusters upgrade <cluster_name> --image-type cos_containerd --zone <compute_zone> --node-pool <node_pool_name> Default Value: Container-optimised OS with containerd (cos_containerd) (default) is the default option for a cluster node image. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/using-containerd 2. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/node-images
input_row.impact: If modifying an existing cluster's Node pool to run COS, the upgrade operation used is long-running and will block other operations on the cluster (including delete) until it has run to completion. COS nodes also provide an option with containerd as the main container runtime directly integrated with Kubernetes instead of docker. Thus, on these nodes, Docker cannot view or access containers or images managed by Kubernetes. Applications should not interact with Docker directly. For general troubleshooting or debugging, use crictl instead.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
This control can be fully verified via GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud). The audit process specifies using the `gcloud container node-pools describe` command to check the image type of node pools, verifying if they use `COS_CONTAINERD` as required. Therefore, this is suitable for automation.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_gke_node_pool_container_optimized_os_enforced

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- **GCP API Calls / Google Cloud SDK Methods:**
  - Use the `gcloud container node-pools describe` command to retrieve the configuration of the node pools in a GKE cluster.
  
- **GCP CLI Command:**
  - `gcloud container node-pools describe <node_pool_name> --cluster <cluster_name> --zone <compute_zone> --format json | jq '.config.imageType'`

- **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
  - Enumerate the GKE clusters and their respective node pools within a project and zone.

- **Exact Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
  - Validate that the `imageType` of each node pool is `COS_CONTAINERD`.

- **Clear Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values:**
  - **Pass:** If the `imageType` for all node pools is `COS_CONTAINERD`.
  - **Fail:** If any node pool has an `imageType` other than `COS_CONTAINERD`.

This automated approach ensures that all node pools in GKE clusters are using Container-Optimized OS as required, thereby reducing the attack surface and improving security through a hardened OS image.
step2_review:
---
## 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: **AGREE**

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:**  
  The classification as "Automated" is correct. The control is fully auditable via GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud). There is no need for manual review, as all required information is available programmatically.

- **GCP SDK/CLI/API Capabilities:**  
  The `gcloud container node-pools describe` command, as well as the underlying GKE REST API (`projects.locations.clusters.nodePools.get`), both expose the `imageType` property for node pools. This allows for automated, scriptable checks across all clusters and node pools in a project.

- **Other GCP Tools:**  
  While GCP Config (Config Connector) could theoretically be used to monitor this, the gcloud CLI and direct API calls are the most straightforward and reliable for this control.

---

## 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: **Score: 8/10**

- **Automation Details:**  
  The provided command is technically correct for a single node pool:
  ```
  gcloud container node-pools describe <node_pool_name> --cluster <cluster_name> --zone <compute_zone> --format json | jq '.config.imageType'
  ```
  However, the assessment omits the need to enumerate all clusters and all node pools within each cluster, which is essential for comprehensive coverage.

- **gcloud Methods:**  
  - To enumerate clusters:  
    `gcloud container clusters list --format="value(name,location)"`
  - To enumerate node pools per cluster:  
    `gcloud container node-pools list --cluster <cluster_name> --zone <zone> --format="value(name)"`
  - To check image type:  
    As above.

- **Program Name:**  
  The program name `gcp_gke_node_pool_container_optimized_os_enforced` is descriptive, but could be more precise (see Improvements).

- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**  
  Correctly defined.

- **Missing:**  
  No mention of regional clusters (`--region`) as well as zonal clusters (`--zone`). GKE supports both, and the audit must account for both.

---

## 3. IMPROVEMENTS

- **Comprehensive Enumeration:**  
  The automation should explicitly include enumeration of all clusters (both zonal and regional) and all node pools within each cluster. This is critical for accuracy in large environments.

- **Command Enhancement:**  
  Sample pseudocode for full automation:
  ```bash
  for region in $(gcloud container clusters list --format="value(location)" | sort -u); do
    for cluster in $(gcloud container clusters list --filter="location=$region" --format="value(name)"); do
      for nodepool in $(gcloud container node-pools list --cluster $cluster --region $region --format="value(name)"); do
        gcloud container node-pools describe $nodepool --cluster $cluster --region $region --format="get(config.imageType)"
      done
    done
  done
  ```
  (Repeat with `--zone` for zonal clusters.)

- **Alternative API:**  
  The REST API method `projects.locations.clusters.nodePools.get` can be used for automation via scripting or GCP client libraries.

- **Program Name Suggestion:**  
  For clarity and convention, consider:  
  `gcp_gke_node_pool_image_type_cos_containerd_enforced`  
  This makes explicit that the check is for the node pool image type and the required value.

- **Edge Cases:**  
  - Consider managed node pools created by third-party tools.
  - Consider node pools in all GCP projects if running at org level.

---

## 4. CONFIDENCE: **HIGH**

- The assessment is technically sound, with minor omissions regarding enumeration and naming conventions.
- The control is fully automatable with GCP APIs and gcloud.

---

## 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: **MODIFY**

- **What to Modify:**  
  - Expand the automation details to explicitly cover enumeration of all clusters (zonal and regional) and all node pools.
  - Update the program name to `gcp_gke_node_pool_image_type_cos_containerd_enforced` for clarity and convention.
  - Add a note to ensure both `--zone` and `--region` are considered in the audit logic.

- **Summary:**  
  The approach is fundamentally correct and automatable, but the automation details should be more comprehensive to ensure complete coverage and clarity.

---

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that the required information (node pool image type) is fully exposed via GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud`). The audit steps in the control map directly to programmatic queries, and enumeration of all clusters and node pools is straightforward using GCP CLI or API. No manual inspection is needed, as all compliance data is accessible and verifiable via automation.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
gcp_gke_node_pool_container_optimized_os_enforced

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP Resource/Service:** Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) Node Pools
- **API/CLI Method:**  
  - Enumerate clusters:  
    `gcloud container clusters list --format="value(name,location)"`
  - Enumerate node pools per cluster:  
    `gcloud container node-pools list --cluster <cluster_name> --region <region> --format="value(name)"`
  - Describe node pool:  
    `gcloud container node-pools describe <node_pool_name> --cluster <cluster_name> --region <region> --format="value(config.imageType)"`
- **Properties to Check:**  
  - `config.imageType` for each node pool
- **Pass Criteria:**  
  - All node pools in all clusters have `config.imageType` set to `COS_CONTAINERD`
- **Fail Criteria:**  
  - Any node pool in any cluster has `config.imageType` set to anything other than `COS_CONTAINERD`

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
There were no substantive disagreements; both AI opinions agreed on automation. The second opinion provided additional detail on the need to enumerate all clusters and node pools, which is included in the final automation details for completeness and accuracy.
